title: SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5, first frontier model since the IPO
subtitle: The 1.5-trillion-parameter V9 model was trained alongside Cursor and undercuts Anthropic's Opus tier on price.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: SpaceXAI released Grok 4.5 yesterday and opened it to the public today, its first frontier model since the company went public at a $1.77 trillion valuation and completed the $60 billion Cursor acquisition in June. Elon Musk described it on X as "an Opus-class model, but faster, more token-efficient and lower cost." Grok 4.5 is now available in Grok Build, in Cursor across all plans, and through the SpaceXAI API console.

The model is built on the 1.5-trillion-parameter V9 foundation and was trained alongside Cursor — the coding editor SpaceXAI now owns — with the explicit goal of a coding and agentic-tasks flagship. Pricing lists at $2 input and $6 output per million tokens, which puts it below both OpenAI's new Sol tier and Anthropic's Opus family. SpaceXAI has not opened EU availability yet, citing pending review; the company expects that to clear in mid-July.

The timing puts Grok 4.5 into the same 48 hours as OpenAI's public GPT-5.6 rollout, restoring the state where every major frontier lab has a current public model at the same time — the first such moment since the Fable 5 export-control ban on June 12. Musk's positioning against Opus is pointed: Anthropic's top tier had been the reference point for agentic coding since Opus 4.8 shipped in May, and pricing Grok 4.5 at roughly one-tenth of Opus reads as an attempt to redraw that comparison, not just enter it.

For learners: this is the first flagship where the training pipeline was co-designed with a specific developer tool from the start, not bolted on afterward. Expect more of that shape — models tuned for the environment they'll actually run in, rather than trained on the open web and adapted later — as labs try to convert vertical integration into measurable capability gains.

sources.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an 'Opus-class model' — TechCrunch
Scoop: Musk's SpaceXAI releases new model, Grok 4.5 — Axios
